content:
The Excitement Around Signals

4 min read
12 hours ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

When Angular introduced Signals, the community responded with excitement.
Developers immediately noticed:

• Simpler reactive state

• Better developer experience

• Cleaner templates

• Fine-grained reactivity

• Less boilerplate
Many teams started migrating existing applications.
Some expected something more than better reactivity.
They expected better architecture.
Unfortunately, those are two very different things.
Not a Member? Read for FREE here.
Better APIs Don’t Automatically Create Better Systems
Imagine two Angular applications.
Both use Signals.
Application A is easy to understand.
Application B is difficult to change.
Why?
Because both applications adopted the same reactive primitive.
Only one adopted good architectural principles.
